tiles.title: Assembly Munich Status 0=station ready to do work, 1=work in progress, 2=work done and good part manufactured, 3=work done and scrap manufactured, 4=station in fault state
Test Munich Status 0=station ready to do work, 1=work in progress, 2=work done and good part manufactured, 3=work done and scrap manufactured, 4=station in fault state
Packaging Munich Status 0=station ready to do work, 1=work in progress, 2=work done and good part manufactured, 3=work done and scrap manufactured, 4=station in fault state
Assembly Seattle Status 0=station ready to do work, 1=work in progress, 2=work done and good part manufactured, 3=work done and scrap manufactured, 4=station in fault state
Test Seattle Status 0=station ready to do work, 1=work in progress, 2=work done and good part manufactured, 3=work done and scrap manufactured, 4=station in fault state
Packaging Seattle Status 0=station ready to do work, 1=work in progress, 2=work done and good part manufactured, 3=work done and scrap manufactured, 4=station in fault state
UNS / ISA-95 Graph
tiles.description: Unified Namespace (UNS) / ISA-95 asset hierarchy rendered as an interactive Plotly node-link graph. Requires the Python plugin on the database.

queries.text:
let tzOffset = toscalar(datetime_utc_to_local(now(), 'Europe/Berlin') - now());
(opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "assembly"
| where DataSetName contains "munich"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "Status"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| extend NodeValue = toint(Value)
| project Timestamp1, NodeValue)
| union (ShiftBreak(_startTime, _endTime, 4, tzOffset))

opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "assembly"
| where DataSetName contains "munich"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "EnergyConsumption"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| order by Timestamp1 desc
| make-series num=avg(todouble(Value)) on Timestamp1 from _startTime to _endTime step 1s 
| extend (anomalies, score, baseline) = series_decompose_anomalies(num, 0.5, -1, 'linefit')

let horizon = (_endTime - _startTime) / 2;
let dt = 1m;
opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "packaging"
| where DataSetName contains "munich"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "NumberOfManufacturedProducts"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| order by Timestamp1 desc
| make-series actual=avg(toint(Value)) on Timestamp1 from _startTime to _endTime + horizon step dt
| extend forecast = series_decompose_forecast(actual, toint(horizon/dt))

let oee = CalculateOEEForStation("assembly", "munich", 6000, _startTime, _endTime);
print round(oee * 100, 2)

let oee = CalculateOEEForStation("test", "munich", 6000, _startTime, _endTime);
print round(oee * 100, 2)

let oee = CalculateOEEForStation("packaging", "munich", 6000, _startTime, _endTime);
print round(oee * 100, 2)

let averageEnergyConsumption = toscalar(
opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "assembly"
| where DataSetName contains "munich"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "EnergyConsumption"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| extend energy = todouble(Value)
| summarize avg(energy));
print round(toscalar(averageEnergyConsumption) * 1000, 2)
print round(CalculateOEEForLine("munich", 6000, _startTime, _endTime) * 100, 2)

opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "assembly"
| where DataSetName contains "seattle"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "Pressure"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| extend NodeValue = toint(Value)
| project Timestamp1, NodeValue

let tzOffset = toscalar(datetime_utc_to_local(now(), 'Europe/Berlin') - now());
(opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "assembly"
| where DataSetName contains "munich"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "ActualCycleTime"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| extend NodeValue = todouble(Value)
| project Timestamp1, NodeValue)
| union (ShiftBreak(_startTime, _endTime, 6000, tzOffset))

let oee = CalculateOEEForStation("assembly", "seattle", 10000, _startTime, _endTime);
print round(oee * 100, 2)

let oee = CalculateOEEForStation("test", "seattle", 10000, _startTime, _endTime);
print round(oee * 100, 2)

| where DataSetName contains "packaging"
| where DataSetName contains "seattle"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "NumberOfManufacturedProducts"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
) on Subject
| order by Timestamp1 desc
| make-series actual=avg(toint(Value)) on Timestamp1 from _startTime to _endTime + horizon step dt
| extend forecast = series_decompose_forecast(actual, toint(horizon/dt))

let compacted =
    opcua_metadata_lkv
    | project levels = pack_array(Enterprise, Site, Area, Line, Workcell, DisplayName)
    | mv-apply with_itemindex = i level = levels to typeof(string) on (
        where isnotempty(level)
        | sort by i asc
        | summarize compact = make_list(level)
    )
    | where array_length(compact) > 0
    | summarize by compactStr = tostring(compact);
let expanded =
    compacted
    | extend compact = todynamic(compactStr)
    | mv-expand with_itemindex = d level = compact to typeof(string)
    | extend d = toint(d), lvl = tostring(level)
    | extend id = strcat_array(array_slice(compact, 0, d), " / ")
    | extend parent = iff(d > 0, strcat_array(array_slice(compact, 0, d - 1), " / "), "");
let nodes =
    expanded
    | summarize by id, label = lvl, depth = d;
let edges =
    expanded
    | where isnotempty(parent)
    | summarize by source = parent, target = id;
let graphData =
    (edges | project rowType = "E", source, target, id = "", label = "", depth = toint(0))
    | union (nodes | project rowType = "V", source = "", target = "", id, label, depth = toint(depth));let pythonCode = ```
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

edges_df = df[df['rowType'] == 'E']
nodes_df = df[df['rowType'] == 'V']

# Build the tree (a forest: one root per top-level ISA-95 branch, e.g. munich and seattle).
children = {}
parent = {}
label = {}
depth = {}
for _, r in nodes_df.iterrows():
    nid = str(r['id'])
    label[nid] = str(r['label'])
    depth[nid] = int(r['depth'])
    children.setdefault(nid, [])
for _, r in edges_df.iterrows():
    s = str(r['source']); t = str(r['target'])
    children.setdefault(s, []); children.setdefault(t, [])
    children[s].append(t)
    parent[t] = s
    label.setdefault(s, s); label.setdefault(t, t)
    depth.setdefault(s, 0); depth.setdefault(t, 0)

roots = sorted([n for n in children if n not in parent], key=lambda n: label.get(n, n))
for n in children:
    children[n] = sorted(children[n], key=lambda c: label.get(c, c))

maxd = max(depth.values()) if depth else 0

# Tidy-tree x assignment: leaves get sequential slots; internal nodes are centred over their children.
xpos = {}
counter = [0.0]
def assign(n):
    ch = children.get(n, [])
    if not ch:
        xpos[n] = counter[0]
        counter[0] += 1.0
        return xpos[n]
    xs = [assign(c) for c in ch]
    xpos[n] = (xs[0] + xs[-1]) / 2.0
    return xpos[n]

gap = 2.0  # blank columns between separate root trees so branches never touch
for r in roots:
    assign(r)
    counter[0] += gap

pos = {n: (xpos[n], float(maxd - depth.get(n, 0))) for n in xpos}
leaf_count = max(counter[0], 1.0)

edge_x = []; edge_y = []
for _, r in edges_df.iterrows():
    s = str(r['source']); t = str(r['target'])
    if s in pos and t in pos:
        x0, y0 = pos[s]; x1, y1 = pos[t]
        edge_x += [x0, x1, None]; edge_y += [y0, y1, None]
edge_trace = go.Scatter(x=edge_x, y=edge_y, mode='lines', line=dict(width=1, color='#888'), hoverinfo='none')

node_x = []; node_y = []; node_text = []; node_hover = []; node_color = []
for n in pos:
    x, y = pos[n]; node_x.append(x); node_y.append(y)
    node_text.append(label.get(n, n)); node_hover.append(n); node_color.append(depth.get(n, 0))
# Leaf labels are dense, so render them vertically and give the deepest tier extra bottom margin.
node_trace = go.Scatter(x=node_x, y=node_y, mode='markers', hovertext=node_hover, hoverinfo='text', marker=dict(size=12, color=node_color, colorscale='Viridis', line=dict(width=1, color='#333')))
# Labels are drawn as annotations (Scatter has no textangle): rotate the dense leaf tier vertically and keep shallow tiers horizontal.
annotations = []
for n in pos:
    ax, ay = pos[n]
    is_leaf = not children.get(n)
    annotations.append(dict(x=ax, y=ay, text=label.get(n, n), showarrow=False, font=dict(size=9), textangle=(90 if is_leaf else 0), xanchor='center', yanchor=('top' if is_leaf else 'bottom'), yshift=(-10 if is_leaf else 10)))

# Scale the figure width with the number of leaves so labels have room to breathe.
fig_width = int(max(1200, min(6000, leaf_count * 34)))
fig = go.Figure(data=[edge_trace, node_trace], layout=go.Layout(title='UNS / ISA-95 asset hierarchy', showlegend=False, hovermode='closest', width=fig_width, height=650, margin=dict(b=140, l=20, r=20, t=40), xaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, range=[-1, leaf_count]), yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, range=[-0.6, maxd + 0.6]), annotations=annotations))
result = pd.DataFrame(data=[fig.to_json()], columns=['plotly'])```;
graphData
| evaluate python(typeof(plotly:string), pythonCode)
| render plotly

opcua_metadata_lkv
| where DataSetName contains "assembly"
| where DataSetName contains "munich"
| join kind=inner (opcua_telemetry
    | where Name == "Pressure"
    | where Timestamp > _startTime and Timestamp < _endTime
